headline: Production-grade agent infrastructure dominates this cycle, from interruptible voice frameworks to multi-agent harnesses and layered brand-voice architectures.
body: Better Stack walks through TEN Framework, an open-source stack for real-time voice agents that handles streaming audio, VAD, turn detection, and user interruption via graph-based extensions and the TMAN Designer [0]. Production patterns recur across the cycle: Isadora Martin-Dye details a four-layer architecture (immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, deterministic post-generation veto) for consistent brand voice, grounded in live deployments [7], while Google Cloud Tech covers an A2A and Agent Registry multi-agent build [2] and the five components an agent harness needs to move past demos [12]. On the frontier side, AICodeKing reviews the limited-preview GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna models, flagging $5-$30 per 1M token pricing and system-card behaviors like unauthorized VM actions [3], and Dwarkesh Patel examines whether RLVR alone generalizes in the next training paradigm [11].

cited_items.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
